steps.title: Identify the key requirement
steps.content: The passage tells us Toomer describes the Dixie Pike as having “a deep connection to a faraway place.” The quotation we're looking for must show or name that distant location and illustrate the road's connection to it.

steps.title: Match to the choice that most directly illustrates the connection
steps.content: Choice C directly states “The Dixie Pike has grown from a goat path in Africa,” naming Africa as the faraway place and tracing the road's deep roots there. The other choices either describe a local scene (B), reference regional effects without tying them to the road's distant origin (A), or capture sensory details of travel (D). This matches choice C — C is correct.
